$schema_note: Fictional, illustrative RBAC assignments for a synthetic, offline-first Microsoft Sentinel lab. NO real GUIDs, subscription IDs, tenant IDs, or secrets. All personas are @contoso.com. All scopes are placeholders: the string 'sub-lab' or the all-zero GUID 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000. See RBAC_REVIEW_MODEL.md and SENTINEL_RBAC_MATRIX.md.

assignments.justification: Tier 1 triage needs to read incidents, analytics rules, workbooks and hunting queries. Read-only floor role; cannot run response actions or change incident ownership.
Read access to underlying tables (SigninLogs, AuditLogs, CyberArk_EPV_CL) for KQL hunting and investigation during triage. Read-only on the data plane.
DRI owns incidents end-to-end during a shift: assign, change status, comment, and run playbooks. Destructive playbook runs still hit the run-time approval gate. Cannot author or edit analytics rules.
Investigation hunting during incident response. Read-only on the data plane.

Authors and code-reviews analytics rules; needs hotfix/rollback ability in an incident. Routine changes still ship through the approval-gated pipeline. PIM-eligible with activation approval rather than a permanent standing grant. Author-not-deployer: does not hold the automation identity.
Build and edit Logic App playbooks in the playbook resource group. Deploy-time capability only; running destructive playbooks is separately gated. PIM-eligible.

Oversight read of incidents and rules. Approver of playbook change gate and destructive-run escalations for Tier-0 assets. Deliberately not a detection author (author != change approver) and holds no Contributor/deploy rights.

Platform ownership: manage security configuration, data connectors and policy state. Broad-reach directory-plane role kept to a single holder and PIM-eligible. Not a day-to-day triage role; holds no workspace Contributor rights.
Standing read of cross-service security posture (Conditional Access, Defender) for platform oversight and DET-003 context. Read-only Entra-plane role.

The ONLY principal that writes analytics rules to the workspace, and only from the pipeline's approval-gated Deploy stage. Non-interactive, no human login. Workspace-scoped; scope creep is a review finding. Credential rotated on schedule; no secret stored in the repo.
Deploys and enables Logic App playbook definitions from the gated pipeline. Resource-group-scoped to the playbook RG only. Non-interactive.

Emergency full control of the detection estate when the automation path is broken and an approver is unreachable during a Critical incident. Held by no working human. Workspace-scoped only (never subscription/tenant Owner). Normally disabled; every enable/sign-in raises a Critical alert and mandates a blameless RCA, credential rotation and re-disable. No credential material stored in the repo.
